---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "80%"
)
```

# BayesEFA: Bayesian Exploratory Factor Analysis

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/RicardoReySaez/BayesEFA/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/RicardoReySaez/BayesEFA/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![Lifecycle: experimental](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-experimental-orange.svg)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html#experimental)
<!-- badges: end -->

<div style="font-size: 1.2em; color: #2C3E50; margin-bottom: 20px;">
  <strong>BayesEFA</strong> provides a flexible, robust, and mathematically sound framework for conducting Bayesian Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA).
</div>

While remaining as intuitive to use as standard frequentist software (like the `psych` package), **BayesEFA** offers the significant mathematical advantage of solving common estimation issues by default, such as non-positive definite correlation matrices and Heywood cases.

---

### ✨ Features at a Glance

<div style="display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 20px; margin-top: 20px; margin-bottom: 30px;">
  
  <div style="flex: 1; min-width: 250px; background: #f8f9fa; padding: 20px; border-radius: 8px; border-left: 4px solid #18BC9C; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);">
    <h4 style="margin-top: 0; color: #2C3E50;">🚀 High Performance</h4>
    <p style="margin-bottom: 0;">Written in underlying highly-optimized C++ via <strong>Stan</strong>. Multi-threaded sampling and fast matrix algebra operations natively supported.</p>
  </div>

  <div style="flex: 1; min-width: 250px; background: #f8f9fa; padding: 20px; border-radius: 8px; border-left: 4px solid #18BC9C; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);">
    <h4 style="margin-top: 0; color: #2C3E50;">🔄 Rotational Indeterminacy</h4>
    <p style="margin-bottom: 0;">Implements the state-of-the-art <strong>Efficient Rotation-Sign-Permutation (E-RSP)</strong> algorithm to resolve rotational invariance issues automatically.</p>
  </div>

  <div style="flex: 1; min-width: 250px; background: #f8f9fa; padding: 20px; border-radius: 8px; border-left: 4px solid #18BC9C; box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);">
    <h4 style="margin-top: 0; color: #2C3E50;">📊 Full Posterior Inference</h4>
    <p style="margin-bottom: 0;">Extract the absolute distribution for any parameter: Bayesian Fit Indices (BSEM), Omega reliabilities, and exact Bayesian Factor Scores.</p>
  </div>

</div>

---

## Installation

### 1. Install `cmdstanr` (Highly Recommended)

While **BayesEFA** natively supports `rstan` out of the box, **we strongly recommend using the `cmdstanr` backend** for significantly faster estimation and lower memory footprint.

Please install `cmdstanr` by following the official guide:

> [**Getting Started with CmdStanR**](https://mc-stan.org/cmdstanr/articles/cmdstanr.html)

### 2. Install BayesEFA

You can install the development version of BayesEFA from [GitHub](https://github.com/RicardoReySaez/BayesEFA) with:

```r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("RicardoReySaez/BayesEFA")
```

Once installed, **BayesEFA** will automatically detect and use `cmdstanr` if it is available on your system.

## Getting Started

For a complete guide on how to perform a Bayesian EFA from scratch, please visit the [Getting Started](articles/getting_started.html) tutorial or explore the comprehensive package website!
